name: Less-common programs
reg: GPM · GEM · land contract · package · ROM

items.text: Two fixed-rate programs deliberately break the level payment. A graduated payment mortgage starts the payments below the true cost — the shortfall is added to the balance, so it negative amortization — then steps them up on a set schedule. A growth equity mortgage does the reverse: the payments run higher than required, the extra goes straight to principal, and the loan pays off early.

A land contract hands over possession but not the deed — the buyer pays the seller directly and takes title only once the agreed sum is paid; walk away early and the payments are forfeit. A package mortgage collateralizes the land, the dwelling, and the personal property inside it — furniture and appliances — as one appraised bundle. A reduction option mortgage is a fixed-rate loan with a one-time chance to drop the rate without refinancing, for a fee.
